name: omarchy_development_tools
content:
Deveopment Toos 
Aternative Editors 
Omarchy ships with Neovim by defaut, but if you'd ike something a bit more mainstream
and famiiar, you can run the Omarchy Menu (Super + Alt + Space) and see the options
under Insta > Editor. We have VSCode, Cursor, Zed, Subime Text, and Heix isted there.
If you don't find what you're ooking for, checkout Insta > Package, and see if it isn't in
an Arch package (and if not, try Insta > AUR to check the AUR.
Theme matching is offered for VSCode , Cursor , and VSCodium .
You can set the system-wide defaut editor under Setup > Defaults. That' open the
UWSM defauts fie, which contros both editor and termina. After you save your change,
you have to reaunch Hyprand for it to take affect (Super + Esc ).
Environment 
Omarchy supports setting up a whoe host of deveopment environments through the
Insta > Deveopment section of the Omarchy Menu (Super + Alt + Space). You' of
course find Ruby on Rais, but aso a three major runtimes for JavaScript Node.js, bun,
Deno), as we as popuar PHP frameworks ike Larave and Symfony. Oh, and there's
.NET, OCama, Zig, and Eixir too. It's a very broad seection!
The majority of these environments are managed by Mise. It's a too that ets you insta
and run mutipe versions of a programming anguage on the same machine. It's ike rbenv
or rvm for Ruby or virtuaenv for Python, but it works for a bunch of different
environments.
To insta, say, Ruby, you'd run mise use -g ruby, which wi both insta Ruby and set it
as the goba defaut. Or, if your project has a .ruby-version fie, you can just run mise i  in
the root of that project.
Docker 
Docker hardy needs any introduction. It aows you to run isoated containers, and
Omarchy instas everything needed to run it we. This incudes Docker itsef, Docker
Compose, and the user group changes needed for you to run Docker as the norma user
and not as root.
Remember to checkout the Lazydocker command to manage your containers in a coo TUI
using Super + Shift + D.
You can setup the common databases for oca deveopment in Docker using Insta >
Deveopment > Docker DB in the Omarchy menu.
GitHub CLI

---

GitHub CLI 
The GitHub CLI et's you authenticate with your GitHub account and cone private
repositories using it. To authenticate, run gh auth login . Then you can checkout private
repositories using gh repo clone org/repo.
You can aso perform a bunch of other GitHub operations using this command. Just run
gh  to see everything that's possibe.
